name: General Tech Trivia
description: Lighter round — computing history, web, hardware and general tech knowledge.

questions.text: Who is often credited as the first computer programmer?
questions.explanation: Ada Lovelace wrote an algorithm for Babbage's Analytical Engine in the 1840s, earning her that title.
HTTP is the protocol browsers and servers use to exchange web pages.

questions.text: How many bits are in a byte?
questions.explanation: A byte is 8 bits, enough to represent 256 distinct values.
Linus Torvalds released the first Linux kernel in 1991 and still oversees its development.

questions.text: The term 'bug' for a computer fault is linked to a real one — what was it?
options: A rare fault in the punch cards
An actual moth found in a relay
A virus spread on magnetic tape
A short circuit in the power supply
questions.explanation: Grace Hopper's team famously taped a moth into a logbook in 1947 after it jammed a relay.
The CPU is the main processor that executes instructions.

options: The request was refused as unauthorised
The server ran into an internal error
The requested resource was not found
questions.explanation: 404 is the classic 'page not found' client error.

questions.text: The Python language is named after…
questions.explanation: Guido van Rossum named it after the British comedy troupe, not the reptile.
A URL is the address that locates a resource on the web.

questions.text: Who is credited with inventing the World Wide Web?
questions.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee proposed and built the Web at CERN around 1989-1991.
RAM is fast, volatile working memory the CPU uses while programs run.
SQL is the standard language for querying and managing relational databases.

questions.text: Which base does the binary number system use?
questions.explanation: Binary uses base 2 — just 0 and 1 — which maps directly to on/off in circuits.
Hex is base 16 (0-9 then A-F), a compact way to write binary — one hex digit is 4 bits.

questions.text: Which company develops the Android operating system?
questions.explanation: Google leads Android development; phone makers build on it.

questions.text: Apple was co-founded by Steve Jobs and…
questions.explanation: Steve Wozniak co-founded Apple with Jobs (and Ronald Wayne) in 1976.

options: Chip prices reliably halve every single year
Software roughly doubles in size every two years
Transistors per chip roughly double every two years
Processor clock speed triples each decade
questions.explanation: Gordon Moore noted the transistor-count doubling trend that drove decades of hardware progress.

questions.text: The default port for HTTPS is…
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses port 443 by default; plain HTTP uses 80.
GPUs excel at parallel work like graphics and, these days, machine learning.

questions.text: In an email address, the '@' separates…
options: The protocol from the host name
The domain from the country code
The first name from the surname
questions.explanation: Everything before @ is the local/user part; after it is the domain.

options: It can only ever run on the Linux system
It is always completely free of any charge
It carries no software licence whatsoever
Its source code is public to view and modify
questions.explanation: Open-source code is published under a licence that permits inspection and modification, e.g. MIT or GPL.
AI refers to systems performing tasks that normally need human intelligence.
USB is the ubiquitous standard for connecting peripherals and transferring data/power.

questions.text: Who created the Git version-control system?
questions.explanation: Linus Torvalds created Git in 2005 to manage Linux kernel development.

questions.text: What does IP stand for in 'IP address'?
questions.explanation: The Internet Protocol handles addressing and routing of packets across networks.

options: Removing unused files from a project
Finding and fixing errors in code
questions.explanation: Debugging is the process of locating and correcting defects.
